ASSIGNED_TO.description: App is assigned to principals approximating accessible users
ASSIGNED_TO.details: If the app does NOT require assignment and has zero explicit assignments, it is broadly reachable (any user can consent/sign-in). Treat as high risk by default.

BROAD_REACHABILITY.description: No assignments but appRoleAssignmentRequired=false implies broad reach
BROAD_REACHABILITY.details: High reachability when assignment required AND assignments are large (direct + group-expanded). Thresholds: >50 (groups-only), >100 (users-only or mixed). Prefer true effective user count via group expansion.

PRIVILEGE.description: Directory roles reachable from app depends on assignments

DECEPTION.description: Unverified publisher with name mismatch between publisher and display name

MIXED_REPLYURL_DOMAINS.identity_laundering_description: Identity laundering signal: reply URLs use domains not aligned with homepage/branding
MIXED_REPLYURL_DOMAINS.attribution_ambiguity_description: Attribution ambiguity: multiple distinct domains in reply URLs
MIXED_REPLYURL_DOMAINS.details: Multiple eTLD+1 values is a weak signal (locales/admin portals are common). Only elevate when an outlier domain is unrelated to primary vendor domain(s).

GOVERNANCE.description: Assignments not required for app governance

UNVERIFIED_PUBLISHER.details: Service principal has no verifiedPublisherId (unverified publisher). Alone is moderate risk; combine with other signals (privileged scopes, broad reachability, URL anomalies).

OFFLINE_ACCESS_PERSISTENCE.details: App requests offline_access (delegated) or otherwise gains refresh-token style persistence. This is persistence risk, not impersonation by itself.

CREDENTIALS_PRESENT.details: Service principal has keyCredentials and/or passwordCredentials present (including 'hidden' secrets where value is not retrievable). Raises persistence and lateral reuse risk.

PASSWORD_CREDENTIALS_PRESENT.details: Service principal has passwordCredentials present. Password secrets are easier to exfiltrate/misuse than certs; treat as higher risk than keyCredentials alone.

IDENTITY_LAUNDERING.details: App appears 'Microsoft-owned' in appOwnerOrganizationId/publisherName (e.g., Microsoft Accounts/Services) while not being a Microsoft first-party app. Indicates ownership attribution is obscured for unverified multi-tenant apps.

REPLYURL_OUTLIER_DOMAIN.details: One or more replyUrls are on a domain that is not in the main vendor domain set (eTLD+1 cluster) for the app. This is stronger than simply 'multiple domains'.

notes.sourceScope: Graph-only scoring. Sign-in logs excluded by design at this stage.
notes.assignments: Assignment reachability should be computed using effective user count via group expansion where possible.
